---
title: "Mapping and analysing public health"
author:
  - name: Ralph Straumann
    url: https://ralphstraumann.ch
date: "2026-04-28 06:04"
image: "national-cancer-institute-NFvdKIhxYlU-unsplash.jpg"
description: "#PublicHealth is a compelling but under-explored domain for #geospatial analysis. The ASIT is hosting a June meetup in Lausanne covering #geomedicine, environmental health influences, physical-activity-friendly #spatialPlanning, and #geostatistics for health risk identification."
---

The ASIT[^asit] organises [an interesting meetup][meetup] on June 18. The 
meetup covers public health and how it can be supported using geoinformation. 
In my perception, this is an under-discussed application domain of geospatial 
information and analysis.[^circle]

The [event][meetup] hosts domain experts who will speak about geomedicine and 
environmental influences on our health, spatial planning that encourages 
physical activity, geostatistics for identifying health risks (using the 
example of dioxin mapping), and the value of geoinformation for public health 
policies.

![Medicine and data: a good match](national-cancer-institute-NFvdKIhxYlU-unsplash.jpg "Medicine and data: a good match")

The [ASIT meetup][meetup] is in-person only (in Lausanne) and seems to 
accomodate about 100 people. Participation is free for ASIT members and 
students. Non-members can attend for a fee of CHF 50.

[^asit]: The Association pour le Système d'Information du Territoire in 
Lausanne, Switzerland, aims to promote geoinformation and facilitate the 
development of geographic data infrastructures by bringing together all 
relevant stakeholders.

[^circle]: It does remind me of the [Health Circle](http://spatialists.ch/posts/2026/03/21-geoinformation-circles-in-geneva/) of the SITG (Système d'Information du Territoire à Genève) in Geneva that I recently 
covered (one out of four topical groups discussing geoinformation applications).

[meetup]: https://asit-asso.ch/prochaine-rencontre-cartographie-sante-publique
